growth_theory.value: Economic development through mega-infrastructure projects that integrate domestic production (agriculture, agroindustry, industry) with international trade via waterway connectivity and port access.

source.quote: to promote agriculture, trade, industry, logistics, we today are launching the construction of the Funan Techo Canal [...] that establishes connection to the Funan empire where Angkor Borei – which was once the capital – focused strongly on agriculture and trade

risk_tolerance.value: High risk tolerance for major infrastructure projects; willing to proceed despite public concerns and skepticism; rejects delay in pursuit of strategic goals.

source.quote: We must make this canal possible at any cost. Why? If we fail to get it done, we will lose the advantage of strengthening our economic potential [...] If we listen to those who are a little worried, we will not accomplish the project.

time_horizon.value: Medium-term operational focus (5-year completion window) embedded in civilizational long-term framing (2,000-year historical continuity and multi-generational legacy).

source.quote: In the next five years, the place will become an economic potential [...] to build a common achievement for the nation that will last for hundreds of years to come

dependency_assumptions.value: Depends on foreign private capital and BOT (Build-Operate-Transfer) investment model; assumes foreign operators will comply with Cambodian law and return infrastructure control post-concession.

source.quote: the private company that borrows money to invest [...] BOT does not mean loss of sovereignty. They build roads, they have the right to operate it and charge, but all are under the Cambodian laws

institution_vs_relationship.value: Relationship and personality-based governance; legitimacy anchored in leader's personal credibility, historical vision, and nationalist narrative rather than institutional rules or transparency mechanisms.

source.quote: I am not going anywhere. Now I say this publicly and nothing is hidden. You can save it. Five more years, if we lose sovereignty, you can come to me anytime [...] my family is not going anywhere

global_positioning_logic.value: Integration into regional trade networks and global commerce while defending national sovereignty and territorial integrity against implied external threats; leverages historical trade precedent to legitimize foreign engagement.

source.quote: we connect transport and trade route from Phnom Penh, provinces along the Tonle Sap and the Mekong River to the sea [...] to promote the prestige, independence, sovereignty and prosperity of Cambodia

consistency_score.value: Moderate-to-high consistency gap: leader states commitment to protecting sovereignty while simultaneously proceeding with foreign-financed BOT project amid public doubts; addresses concerns rhetorically but dismisses them operationally.

source.quote: Although some of you have the right to express concern, we must give justice to all – both those who see economic gain from the project, and the answers to those who (are) worried about losing their land and sovereignty [...] which we must complete
